description: A full-length icon-style portrait of St. Sergius of Radonezh depicted as an elderly monk with a long white beard and a golden halo. He wears a dark monastic robe and mantle with a cross-patterned stole. His right hand is raised in a blessing gesture while his left hand holds a long scroll with Slavonic text. Behind him a green landscape with birch trees and a white monastery complex is visible. A small icon of the Trinity is mounted in the upper right corner. The panel is framed by an ornate floral and geometric border in gold and earth tones.
theological_description: St. Sergius of Radonezh (1314-1392) was a revered Russian monk and mystic who founded the Trinity Lavra monastery. Though a post-biblical figure, his life embodies biblical ideals of monastic withdrawal, prayer, and humility. The blessing hand and scroll signify his teaching authority. The small Trinity icon in the corner references his deep devotion to the Holy Trinity, recalling the Trinitarian theophanies of Scripture. His life mirrors the call to deny oneself and follow Christ, living out the contemplative and ascetic tradition rooted in the Desert Fathers' reading of the Gospels.
